text: "Hello?" "Hello?" "Good afternoon. This is Jacob. So, I'm calling because there are pending grocery and cash benefits worth up to $300 available on your Medicare account through North Carolina state relief program. So far, have you received any updates or notifications about this yet?" "No, I haven't." "Oh, that's alright. That's exactly why I'm calling. And I want to help you get these benefits processed before they go unclaimed. You're covered under both part A and part B, yes?" "Yes." "Perfect." "You qualified for the benefit." "I'm boarding you to our supervisor. Please hold on." "Hi, thank you." "No, don't watch both of your line. My name is Eric Wilson. How are you?" "I'm fine. I was trying to tell him I'm with United Healthcare." "Okay. Anything else you want to tell me?" "No, I mean, it's just I'm with United Healthcare and I give off a benefit card from them." "Well, I understand that you have United Healthcare. We do work with United Healthcare. We do work with 25 average companies. But the thing is that I'm here. I'm here just talking about the Medicare benefits. You can get that with the same insurance." "Right." "Yes, ma'am." "Okay." "So, this conversation not effective your current plan. I'm not going to mess up your video in other healthcare. And as you know, it's about your Medicare-Spanish plan. So, we are just here to help you to qualify some new extra benefits. But if it was your age now, how old are you?" "Sorry?" "60." "Okay. And do you make your own decisions with your healthcare?" "Yes, really." "Okay. That's my meeting, taking care of yourself. And have you served in a military army Navy like do you have, try careful life or VA benefits?" "No." "No. Right. And you said you're getting some kind of money for food and gross business on your U card, right?" "Right." "Okay. And how much you're getting? Is it more than $200 or less than that?" "There's more." "More than that. Okay. So, now I'm actually the maximum amount on your food and gross business alone. It's $385. So, if I can help you to increase the limit, it might be you can get more than $300. So, that's going to be helping you, right?" "I'd get three thousand more." "Okay. I got you. And do you have help through the state as well like Medicaid along with your Medicare.
